text: When you are learning to code, bugs are inevitable. The first step is identifying bugs by carefully reading error messages and checking what your program actually does versus what you expected. A systematic approach saves time: instead of guessing, isolate the problem by testing one small part at a time. Modern debugging tools like breakpoints and step-through execution are very helpful, as they let you pause the code and inspect variable values. Finally, logging—adding print statements to show what is happening at key points—can reveal hidden issues. The key is to stay calm and follow a clear process.
